rejected_candidates.title: Revert callback handlers trust revertMessage length and can self-revert on malformed payloads
rejected_candidates.reason: Protocol-generated revert messages in these paths always include at least the 32-byte externalId; the short-message condition requires malformed trusted-gateway input and is not a realistic standalone exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.onCall returns 0x00000000 success code, risking callback status mismatch
rejected_candidates.reason: No gateway-side magic return-value check was identified; claim is speculative and does not show a concrete inconsistent-finality exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: onCall handlers do not authenticate source contract/chain in MessageContext
rejected_candidates.reason: These endpoints are permissionless by design; absence of source allowlisting is not independently exploitable beyond already-reported payload/accounting validation bugs.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative.claimRefund emits zeroed token/amount after deleting storage
rejected_candidates.reason: Valid informational logging defect, but no direct fund-loss, theft, insolvency, or liveness impact.

rejected_candidates.title: Slippage parameter is configurable but never enforced on swaps
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: slippage feeds `amountInMax` for `swapTokensForExactTokens`; a separate post-check bug is already captured in F-022.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.depositAndCall lacks slippage and minimum output validation
rejected_candidates.reason: The DODO router call includes and enforces `minReturnAmount`; no missing-min-output exploit was demonstrated.

rejected_candidates.title: Platform fee can be set to zero, enabling owner fund extraction
rejected_candidates.reason: Setting fee to zero is a governance/configuration choice, not a concrete exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.onCall lacks access control allowing anyone to drain user tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Mischaracterized: `onCall` is `onlyGateway`; stated arbitrary-caller path is invalid.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayTransferNative.withdrawToNativeChain uses nominal amount for fee calculation before swap
rejected_candidates.reason: Charging fee on input amount is explicit fee policy, not a security vulnerability by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewayCrossChain.onCall deducts platform fee before swap, reducing swap efficiency
rejected_candidates.reason: Economic design preference, not a protocol-security bug.

rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend.depositAndCall does not bind output token to destination chain asset
rejected_candidates.reason: As stated, this is primarily a user-input/supportability issue; reserve-drain consequences from asset/output mismatch are already covered by F-010.
